# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/x/denokv@0.54.1"; // denopy https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more details on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts"; 
// For HTTP Server implementation check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm    denopy Denpy from "https://deno.land/x/denopack@0.15.2";
import { parseParams } from 'https://deno.land/std/flags/mod.ts'; // For handling command line parameters check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'https://deno.land/x/uuid/mod.ts'; // For UUID implementation check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
import { createRouter } from 'https://deno.land/x/dhttp/mod.ts';  // For creating dynamic routing check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
import { createKv } from 'https://deno.land/x/dhttp@0.54.1'; // For creating a key-value store check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
import { open } from 'https://deno.land/std/fs/mod.ts'; // For file handling with Denops check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm

import { parseISO } from 'https://deno.land/x/date-fns@v2.18.9/mod.ts' // For handling date parsing check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
import { v } from 'https://deno.land/std/_deriverrors_tsdecorators';  // For decorating and handling functions errors check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
import { z } from 'https://deno.land/_modules_tsdecorators';  // For decorating functions with custom error messages check https://github.com/DenisMoretti/DenoKit#readme for more information on how to use it in your project, or you can install via npm
